_provenance.note: Bug-tier lifecycle demonstration. Before R4, 5886 got a bare exempt-PASS (persisted REVIEW_RESULT runner=exempt, llm_calls=0). Under the bug tier it now gets a LIGHT ADVISORY review = DET floor + the necessity probe, never blocking. 5886 is a well-motivated absent-capability bug (reproduction + Expected/Actual present), so the necessity probe correctly does NOT fire — it is the bug-tier lifecycle fixture, NOT an over-action necessity positive. Run with --no-sign --source local so no attestation is written to the closed ticket.
_provenance.pass_condition: verdict PASS, runner != exempt (a substantive review ran, not the bare exempt short-circuit), no blocking (a bug is never blocked), coverage.det.bug_tier True.

interpretation: The bug tier replaced the bare exempt-PASS with a substantive advisory review (runner=pydantic_ai, llm_ran=True) that never blocks the bug (blocking=0). The necessity probe was clean on 5886 (well-motivated), the correct outcome.
